title: Luna Lovegood and Rei Ayanami
wikitext:
[]: Luna Lovegood and Rei Ayanami             Quote Post by V » Fri Jul 13, 2007 11:44 am ok the connection is tenuous at best, but I never read the Harry Potter books... ...however I just got out of watching Harry Potter 5, and I think the Luna Lovegood character was really cool and stole every scene she was in. And I just went "wow, I wonder if a live-action Rei would be somewhat similar to that" Ok the connection is tenuous at best, and I'm being half-serious. And really....Loony actually was a lot more outgoing than Rei ever would be (Luna carries on actual one on one conversations with Harry, which she actually *initiates*, and really Rei isn't like that) still she got the....."dreamy/spacey not really in the room with everyone else" thing down. Probably I think it's because she never made eye contact with anyone. I mean ever. She'd turn her head a bit in the direction of people she was talking to but always seemed kind of half there by not looking at them (like Rei does) well just being goofy, just that I saw HP5 last night with the family (note; I'm not a big HP fan; just see the movies when they're out. Diehard LOTR fan :) )

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Jul 13, 2007 1:22 pm According to my brother, who just saw the movie, Film Luna is to Book Luna what Manga Asuka is to Anime Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Fri Jul 13, 2007 1:23 pm I can't actually remember Luna very well, but i can remember her mannerisms, mainly because i used to be exactly like that when i was younger. :oops: I think it would fit very well with a new Rei.

[]: Re: Luna Lovegood and Rei Ayanami             Quote Post by Malichinari » Fri Jul 13, 2007 5:06 pm V wrote: Diehard LOTR fan :)

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 13, 2007 8:32 pm BOOOOOOOOOO! How can you compare those two? Luna's actually pretty cheery, and no matter how out of place or dreamy she might be, she's actually a very pleasant character. Rei is very, very dark, depressed to the point of being suicidal, horrendously introverted, doesn't understand emotions or social interactions at all, and is horrified by the darkness within herself- the mind and soul of Lilith. Although Rei has her bright moments(Like her smiling for the first time).

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Jul 13, 2007 10:38 pm Heh. Luna's probably my favorite Harry Potter character; it's been going downhill, and i've been following it ever since the first book was released (IE: Before it was cool), so why stop now? She's one of the good things that came of the later books.

[]:             Quote Post by Vane » Sun Jul 15, 2007 5:21 pm I find Luna and Rei pretty different. Luna is spacey, but the only reason that she doesn't really socialize much with people is because everyone thinks she is a freak(though she really isn't a freak, she just really... acceptant of anything.), reading the book gives you a clearer insight on the character. Luna is actually pretty normal compared to Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 16, 2007 12:29 am freak isnt the word i would use, Luna is more......Loony. Loony Luna she is very spacey, where Rei on the other hand is just empty Luna is out there, Rei is just very deep within herself so no, not much relevance there but i think i get what your saying, i dont know how to put it in words, but i think i have an idea

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jul 16, 2007 3:32 am as I understand it, book-Luna was very different from move-Luna. Book-Luna was apparently kind of like Alternate Universe Rei from episode 26. But what I was saying was just "wow, the actress they have doing movie-Luna does this REALLY good "calm and detached voice" which reminded me of Rei...a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Mon Jul 16, 2007 4:47 am NO NO NO NO NO

[]:             Quote Post by DarkNova » Mon Jul 16, 2007 10:04 am O.K Iâ€™ll admit I havenâ€™t seen the move yet but if the move Luna has anything in common with the book Luna then I would NOT EVER want an actress who played her well to play Rei in the LAM, at lest not based on that. P.S Was the Harry Potter move any good, or did it skip almost all the book like 4 did?

[]:             Quote Post by Vane » Mon Jul 16, 2007 10:14 am DarkNova wrote: Was the Harry Potter move any good, or did it skip almost all the book like 4 did?
